I2C driver interface for initialization, usage, reset, and register read/write operations.

# Purpose
This C header file provides an interface for managing I2C communication with devices. It includes function declarations for initializing the I2C driver ([`i2c_init`](<#i2c_init>)), starting and stopping the use of the I2C bus with a specific device ([`i2c_use`](<#i2c_use>) and [`i2c_release`](<#i2c_release>)), and resetting the bus ([`i2c_reset`](<#i2c_reset>)). The file also provides functions for manual clock recovery ([`i2c_bitbang_recovery`](<#i2c_bitbang_recovery>)), reading from and writing to device registers ([`i2c_read_register`](<#i2c_read_register>), [`i2c_read_register_block`](<#i2c_read_register_block>), [`i2c_write_register`](<#i2c_write_register>), and [`i2c_write_register_block`](<#i2c_write_register_block>)). Each function requires a device identifier (`I2cDevice`) and, for read and write operations, additional parameters such as device addresses and data buffers. The functions return a boolean value indicating the success or failure of the operation.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `board/board.h`
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`
